title: afrodaphne gaboonensis
intro: Afrodaphne gaboonensis, also known as the Gaboon cinnamon or African cinnamon, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of West Africa. The plant's bark has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Afrodaphne gaboonensis include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and support digestive health. Afrodaphne gaboonensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
